d-finding.text: Synthesised from the study results, the following guidelines represent minima — not targets. Exceeding them is nearly always better.

d-desktop.content:
### Desktop / Wide Viewport

**Body size** 15–18px

**Weight** 400 for body; 300 only for display sizes above 28px

**Line height** 1.5–1.65 for body; 1.2–1.35 for headings

**Line length** 60–75 characters

**Contrast** minimum 4.5:1 for body text; 3:1 for large text (18px bold+)

**Paragraph spacing** 0.75–1× line height between paragraphs

**Note:** Dark mode requires separate validation — the optimal values differ slightly from light mode defaults due to halation effects at high contrast ratios.

d-mobile.content:
### Mobile / Narrow Viewport

**Body size** 16–18px (never below 16px)

**Weight** 400 for body; 300 weight shows measurable legibility loss below 30 lux

**Line height** 1.6–1.7 for body text

**Line length** 45–60 characters; use responsive text containers, not fixed widths

**Contrast** 4.5:1 minimum; 7:1 preferred for outdoor-use contexts

**Touch target** Minimum 44×44px for any interactive text

**Note:** Users read more slowly on mobile — 25% slower on average in controlled conditions. Prioritise clarity over information density.
